source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8089_Passage2 We Have Star Performers!.html

passages.title: We Have Star Performers!
passages.source_html:
We Have Star Performers! 


A 


The difference between companies is people. With capital and technology in plentiful supply, the critical resource for companies in the knowledge era will be human talent. Companies full of achievers will, by definition, outperform organisations of plodders. Ergo, compete ferociously for the best people. Poach and pamper stars; ruthlessly weed out second-raters. This, in essence, has been the recruitment strategy of the ambitious company of the past decade. The ‘talent mindset’ was given definitive form in two reports by the consultancy McKinsey famously entitled ‘The War for Talent’. Although the intensity of the warfare subsequently subsided along with the air in the internet bubble, it has been warming up again as the economy tightens: labour shortages, for example, are the reason the government has laid out the welcome mat for immigrants from the new Europe. 


B


Yet while the diagnosis – people are important – is evident to the point of platitude, the apparently logical prescription – hire the best – like so much in management is not only not obvious: it is in fact profoundly wrong. The first suspicions dawned with the crash to earth of the dotcom meteors, which showed that dumb is dumb whatever the IQ of those who perpetrate it. The point was illuminated in brilliant relief by Enron, whose leaders, as a New Yorker article called ‘The Talent Myth’ entertainingly related, were so convinced of their own cleverness that they never twigged that collective intelligence is not the sum of a lot of individual intelligence. In fact, in a profound sense, the two are opposites. Enron believed in stars, noted author Malcolm Gladwell, because they didn’t believe in systems. But companies don’t just create: ‘they execute and compete and coordinate the efforts of many people, and the organisations that are most successful at that task are the ones where the system is the star’. The truth is that you can’t win the talent wars by hiring stars – only lose it. New light on why this should be so is thrown by an analysis of star behaviour in this months’ Harvard Business Review. In a study of the careers of 1,000 star-stock analysts in the 1990s, the researchers found that when a company recruited a star performer, three things happened. 


C


First, stardom doesn’t easily transfer from one organisation to another. In many cases, performance dropped sharply when high performers switched employers and in some instances never recovered. More of success than commonly supposed is due to the working environment - systems, processes, leadership, accumulated embedded learning that are absent in and can’t be transported to the new firm. Moreover, precisely because of their past stellar performance,stars were unwilling to learn new tricks and antagonised those (on whom they now unwittingly depended) who could teach them. So they moved, upping their salary as they did – 36 per cent moved on within three years, fast even for Wall Street. Second, group performance suffered as a result of tensions and resentment by rivals within the team. One respondent likened hiring a star to an organ transplant. The new organ can damage others by hogging the blood supply, other organs can start aching or threaten to stop working or the body can reject the transplants altogether, he said. ‘You should think about it very carefully before you do [a transplant] to a healthy body.’ Third, investors punished the offender by selling its stock. This is ironic, since the motive for importing stars was often a suffering share price in the first place. Shareholders evidently believe that the company is overpaying, the hiree is cashing in on a glorious past rather than preparing for a glowing present, and a spending spree is in the offing. 


D


The result of mass star hirings as well as individual ones seems to confirm such doubts. Look at County NatWest and Barclays de Zoete Wedd, both of which hired teams of stars with loud fanfare to do great things in investment banking in the 1990s. Both failed dismally. Everyone accepts the cliche that people make the organisation – but much more does the organisation make the people. When researchers studied the performance of fund managers in the 1990s, they discovered that just 30 per cent of variation in fund performance was due to the individual, compared to 70 per cent to the company-specific setting. 


E 


That will be no surprise to those familiar with systems thinking. W. Edwards Deming used to say that there was no point in beating up on people when 90 per cent of performance variation was down to the system within which they worked. Consistent improvement, he said, is a matter not of raising the level of individual intelligence, but of the learning of the organisation as a whole. The star system is glamorous - for the few. But it rarely benefits the company that thinks it is working it. And the knock-on consequences indirectly affect everyone else too. As one internet response to Gladwell’s New Yorker article put it: after Enron, ‘the rest of corporate America is stuck with overpaid, arrogant, underachieving, and relatively useless talent.’ 


F 


Football is another illustration of the stars vs systems strategic choice. As with investment banks and stockbrokers, it seems obvious that success should ultimately be down to money. Great players are scarce and expensive. So the club that can afford more of them than anyone else will win. But the performance of Arsenal and Manchester United on one hand and Chelsea and Real Madrid on the other proves that it’s not as easy as that. While Chelsea and Real have the funds to be compulsive star collectors – as with Juan Sebastian Veron – they are less successful than Arsenal and United which, like Liverpool before them, have put much more emphasis on developing a setting within which stars-in-the-making can flourish. Significantly,Thierry Henry, Patrick Veira and Robert Pires are much bigger stars than when Arsenal bought them, their value ( in all senses) enhanced by the Arsenal system. At Chelsea, by contrast, the only context is the stars themselves – managers with different outlooks come and go every couple of seasons. There is no settled system for the stars to blend into. The Chelsea context has not only not added value, it has subtracted it. The side is less than the sum of its exorbitantly expensive parts. Even Real Madrid’s galacticos,the most extravagantly gifted on the planet, are being outperformed by less talented but better-integrated Spanish sides. In football, too, stars are trumped by systems. 


G 


So if not by hiring stars, how do you compete in the war for talent? You grow your own. This worked for investment analysts, where some companies were not only better at creating stars but also at retaining them. Because they had a much more sophisticated view of the interdependent relationship between star and system, they kept them longer without resorting to the exorbitant salaries that were so destructive to rivals.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-17 


The passage has seven paragraphs A-G. Which paragraph contains the following information? Write the correct letter A-G, in boxes 14-17 on your answer sheet.


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 One example from non-commerce/business settings that better system wins bigger stars.
✅ 正确答案：F

原文答案句：Football is another illustration of the stars vs systems strategic choice. As with investment banks and stockbrokers, it seems obvious that success should ultimately be down to money. Great players are scarce and expensive. So the club that can afford more of them than anyone else will win. But the performance of Arsenal and Manchester United on one hand and Chelsea and Real Madrid on the other proves that it’s not as easy as that. While Chelsea and Real have the funds to be compulsive star collectors – as with Juan Sebastian Veron – they are less successful than Arsenal and United which, like Liverpool before them, have put much more emphasis on developing a setting within which stars-in-the-making can flourish. Significantly, Thierry Henry, Patrick Veira and Robert Pires are much bigger stars than when Arsenal bought them, their value ( in all senses) enhanced by the Arsenal system. At Chelsea, by contrast, the only context is the stars themselves – managers with different outlooks come and go every couple of seasons. There is no settled system for the stars to blend into. The Chelsea context has not only not added value, it has subtracted it. The side is less than the sum of its exorbitantly expensive parts. Even Real Madrid’s galacticos, the most extravagantly gifted on the planet, are being outperformed by less talented but better-integrated Spanish sides. In football, too, stars are trumped by systems.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“一个非商业/商业环境中的例子，更好的系统赢得了更大的明星”。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“非商业/商业环境”和“更好的系统赢得更大的明星”。在文章中，只有F段提到了非商业环境（足球），并且明确指出更好的系统（如阿森纳和曼联的系统）能够使明星球员的价值得到提升，而仅仅依赖明星球员的俱乐部（如切尔西和皇家马德里）却表现不佳。

解题思路：根据题目中的关键词，我们可以在文章中定位到F段。F段以足球为例，说明在非商业环境中，更好的系统能够使明星球员的价值得到提升，而仅仅依赖明星球员的俱乐部却表现不佳。这与题目中的“更好的系统赢得更大的明星”完全吻合，因此F段是正确答案。
15

15 One failed company that believes stars rather than system.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：The point was illuminated in brilliant relief by Enron, whose leaders, as a New Yorker article called ‘The Talent Myth’ entertainingly related, were so convinced of their own cleverness that they never twigged that collective intelligence is not the sum of a lot of individual intelligences.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问的是“一个相信明星而非系统的失败公司”。  

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"failed company"、"stars rather than system"定位到B段，其中提到"Enron"是一个相信明星的公司。  

解题思路：B段明确提到Enron的领导者们相信自己的聪明才智，却没有意识到集体智慧不等于个体智慧的简单相加，这表明Enron是一个相信明星而非系统的公司。同时，Enron在文中被描述为一个失败的案例，符合题目的要求。
16

16 One suggestion that author made to acquire employees then to win the competition nowadays.
✅ 正确答案：G

原文答案句：You grow your own. This worked for investment analysts, where some companies were not only better at creating stars but also at retaining them.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是作者提出了哪些建议来获得员工以赢得当今的竞争。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"acquire employees"和"win the competition"可以定位到G段，该段提到了"how do you compete in the war for talent"以及具体的建议。

解题思路：根据原文G段中的句子"You grow your own. This worked for investment analysts, where some companies were not only better at creating stars but also at retaining them."可以确定，作者建议通过培养自己的员工来获得人才，而不是仅仅通过招聘明星员工。这与题目所问的"获得员工以赢得竞争"的建议相符。因此，答案为G。
17

17 One metaphor to human medical anatomy that illustrates the problems of hiring stars.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：One respondent likened hiring a star to an organ transplant. The new organ can damage others by hogging the blood supply, other organs can start aching or threaten to stop working or the body can reject the transplant altogether, he said. ‘You should think about it very carefully before you do [a transplant] to a healthy body.’

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是哪一段包含了一个关于人体医学解剖的比喻，用来说明雇佣明星员工所带来的问题。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"metaphor"、"human medical anatomy"和"problems of hiring stars"，可以在文章中找到对应的比喻，即C段中提到的"an organ transplant"的比喻。

解题思路：C段中提到了一个比喻，将雇佣明星员工比作人体器官移植。这个比喻形象地说明了雇佣明星员工可能会带来的问题，比如新员工可能会损害其他员工（如同新器官损害其他器官一样），或者整个团队可能会排斥新员工（如同身体排斥移植的器官一样）。因此，C段包含了题目所要求的比喻。

Questions 18-21 


Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage 1? In boxes 18-21 on your answer sheet, write YES if the statement agrees with the information NO if the statement contradicts the information NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this


18

McKinsey who wrote ‘The War for Talent’ had not expected the huge influence made by this book.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：Although the intensity of the warfare subsequently subsided along with the air in the internet bubble, it has been warming up again as the economy tightens: labour shortages, for example, are the reason the government has laid out the welcome mat for immigrants from the new Europe.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问麦肯锡公司撰写《人才战争》这本书时，是否预料到这本书会产生巨大的影响。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"The War for Talent"和"McKinsey"定位到A段第二句，提到麦肯锡撰写的《人才战争》这本书。

解题思路：原文中提到麦肯锡撰写了《人才战争》这本书，但并未提及他们是否预料到这本书会产生巨大的影响。因此，根据原文信息，无法确定麦肯锡是否预料到这本书的影响，所以答案为NOT GIVEN。


19

Economic condition becomes one of the factors which decide whether or not a country would prefer to hire foreign employees.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：As the economy tightens: labour shortages, for example, are the reason the government has laid out the welcome mat for immigrants from the new Europe.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是经济状况是否成为决定一个国家是否愿意雇佣外国员工的一个因素。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"economic condition"和"hire foreign employees"来定位答案。

解题思路：原文A段提到，随着经济收紧，劳动力短缺，例如，是政府为来自新欧洲的移民铺开欢迎垫的原因。这说明经济状况（经济收紧）是导致劳动力短缺的一个因素，而劳动力短缺又促使政府雇佣外国员工。因此，经济状况确实是一个决定因素。所以答案是YES。


20

The collapse of Enron is caused totally by a unfortunate incident instead of company’s management mistake.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：The point was illuminated in brilliant relief by Enron, whose leaders, as a New Yorker article called ‘The Talent Myth’ entertainingly related, were so convinced of their own cleverness that they never twigged that collective intelligence is not the sum of a lot of individual intelligences.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问“Enron的倒闭完全是由不幸事件造成的，而不是公司管理失误”，需要判断这个说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：题目中的"Enron"和"collapse"（倒闭）是关键词，可以定位到B段中关于Enron的描述。

解题思路：原文提到Enron的领导层过于自信，没有意识到集体智慧不等于个体智慧的简单相加，这表明Enron的失败与公司管理理念（即过度依赖明星员工而忽视系统）有关，而非单纯的不幸事件。因此，题目说法与原文信息矛盾，答案为NO。


21

Football clubs that focus making stars in the setting are better than simply collecting stars

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：As with investment banks and stockbrokers, it seems obvious that success should ultimately be down to money. Great players are scarce and expensive. So the club that can afford more of them than anyone else will win. But the performance of Arsenal and Manchester United on one hand and Chelsea and Real Madrid on the other proves that it’s not as easy as that. While Chelsea and Real have the funds to be compulsive star collectors – as with Juan Sebastian Veron – they are less successful than Arsenal and United which, like Liverpool before them, have put much more emphasis on developing a setting within which stars-in-the-making can flourish.

对应段落：F段

审题：题目问的是“专注于在环境中培养明星的足球俱乐部比单纯收集明星的俱乐部更好”这一陈述是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“Football clubs”、“making stars in the setting”和“better than simply collecting stars”定位到F段中关于足球俱乐部成功因素的对比。

解题思路：F段明确提到，像切尔西和皇家马德里这样的俱乐部虽然拥有资金来收集明星球员（如胡安·塞巴斯蒂安·维埃拉），但它们的成功程度不如阿森纳和曼联，后者更注重培养一个能让潜力明星球员茁壮成长的环境。原文直接对比了两种策略的效果，表明“注重环境培养”的策略更为成功，因此题目陈述与原文信息一致。


Questions 22-26 


Complete the summary below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 22-26 on your answer sheet.


22

An investigation carried out on 1,000 22 . 

正确答案： 22 Analysts##star-stock analysts
Question 22

原文答案句：In a study of the careers of 1,000 star-stock analysts in the 1990s, the researchers found that when a company recruited a star performer, three things happened.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目要求根据文章内容填写一个关于研究对象职业的描述，具体是哪类职业人员。

定位关键信息：题目中的数字"1,000"和职业相关描述是定位关键词，在文章中找到对应的句子。

解题思路：根据原文句子"In a study of the careers of 1,000 star-stock analysts in the 1990s"可以直接确定研究对象是"star-stock analysts"，即明星股票分析师。题目中的"22 %"可能是题目格式错误，实际需要填写的是职业名称"star-stock analysts"。


23

Participants of a survey by Harvard Business Review found a company hire a 23  has negative effects.

正确答案： 23 performance star##star##star performer
Question 23

原文答案句：In a study of the careers of 1,000 star-stock analysts in the 1990s, the researchers found that when a company recruited a star performer, three things happened.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问的是哈佛商业评论的调查参与者发现公司雇佣明星员工的比例是多少。  

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"Harvard Business Review"和"company hire"可以定位到C段，找到关于哈佛商业评论调查的内容。  

解题思路：原文提到哈佛商业评论对1000名明星股票分析师的职业生涯进行了研究，发现当公司雇佣明星员工时会发生三件事。hire和recruit是同意替换，所以填star performer。


24

For instance, they behave considerably worse in a new team than in the 24 that they used to be. 


正确答案： 24 working environment##settings
Question 24

原文答案句：In many cases, performance dropped sharply when high performers switched employers and in some instances never recovered. More of success than commonly supposed is due to the working environment - systems, processes, leadership, accumulated embedded learning that are absent in and can’t be transported to the new firm.（C段）

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是明星员工在新团队中的表现比在原来的环境中差的原因。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"behave considerably worse"和"new team"定位到C段，找到描述明星员工表现下降的句子。

解题思路：原文提到"More of success than commonly supposed is due to the working environment - systems, processes, leadership, accumulated embedded learning that are absent in and can’t be transported to the new firm."，说明成功更多归因于工作环境，而这些因素在新公司中是缺失的。因此，明星员工在新团队中表现差是因为新公司缺乏原有的"working environment"或"settings"。


25

They move faster than Wall Street and increase their 25 .

正确答案： 25 salary
Question 25

原文答案句：So they moved, upping their salary as they did – 36 per cent moved on within three years, fast even for Wall Street.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目要求填写员工离职时增加的具体内容，并提到他们比华尔街更快，增加了25%。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"move faster than wall street"和"25 "可以定位到C段描述明星员工离职时的情况。

解题思路：原文C段提到明星员工离职时会"upping their salary"，即提高他们的薪水，并具体说明"36 per cent moved on within three years, fast even for Wall Street"，这与题目描述的"move faster than wall street"和增加25%相吻合。因此，答案为"salary"。


26

Secondly, they faced rejections or refuse from those 26 within the team. Lastly, the one who made mistakes had been punished by selling his/her stock share.

正确答案： 26 rivals
Question 26

原文答案句：One respondent likened hiring a star to an organ transplant. The new organ can damage others by hogging the blood supply, other organs can start aching or threaten to stop working or the body can reject the transplant altogether, he said. ‘You should think about it very carefully before you do [a transplant] to a healthy body.’

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是“其次，他们面临来自团队内部的拒绝或排斥”，需要填入被拒绝或排斥的对象。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“rejections or refuse”和“within the team”，通过这些词可以定位到C段中关于团队内部排斥的描述。

解题思路：根据原文中的句子“other organs can start aching or threaten to stop working or the body can reject the transplant altogether, he said.”，可以理解为团队内部的“other organs”对应的是“rivals”，即竞争对手或对手。因此，填入“rivals”符合题意。
